name: Bernice Herb's Corn Fritters
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook 3 ears of corn in a heavy skillet over moderately low heat until crisp.
2. Transfer the cooked bacon to paper towels to drain and reserve the bacon fat in a heatproof bowl.
3. Grate the remaining corn using the largest holes of a 4-sided grater until you have about half a cup.
4. Beat one large egg and add it to the grated corn.
5. Stir in three tablespoons of all-purpose flour, five and a half tablespoons of sugar, one teaspoon of baking powder, and half a teaspoon of salt with freshly ground black pepper to taste until well combined.
1. In a separate skillet, heat one tablespoon of reserved bacon fat over moderate heat.
2. Form fritters by dropping about one-eighth cup of batter into the skillet for each.
3. Cook the fritters until they are golden brown on each side, about 1 to 2 minutes per side.
4. Serve the fritters warm with bacon and syrup or tomatoes. Makes about six fritters, serving two or three as a side dish.
Accompaniment: pure maple syrup or sliced tomatoes
